task:
Build this app in Clear:

**Internal Request Queue** (Level 8)

Build an internal request queue for IT/HR/Facilities/Finance tickets. Employees submit a request (title, description, category, priority, submitter). Ops staff can update requests (requires login) — change status, assign to a team, add resolution notes. Need: POST /api/requests (public), GET /api/requests (list), GET /api/requests/new (filter by status='new'), PUT /api/requests/:id (auth required).

Starting skeleton:
```clear
build
final_source:
build for web and javascript backend

create a Requests table:
  title, required
  description
  category, default 'IT'
  priority, default 'Normal'
  submitter, required
  status, default 'new'
  assigned_to
  resolution_notes

allow signup and login
owner is 'ops@example.com'

# Endpoints

when user sends request to /api/requests:
  validate request:
    title is text, required
    submitter is text, required
  saved = save request to Requests
  send back saved with status 201

when user calls GET /api/requests:
  requests = get all Requests
  send back requests

when user calls GET /api/requests/new:
  new_requests = get all Requests where status is 'new'
  send back new_requests

when user calls PUT /api/requests/:id:
  requires login
  changes = look up Requests with this id
  if changes has no id:
    send back 'Request not found' with status 404
  updated = save changes to Requests
  send back updated

# Frontend Pages

page 'Submit Request' at '/':
  title = ''
  description = ''
  category = 'IT'
  priority = 'Normal'
  submitter = ''
  
  section 'Employee Request Form':
    'Title' as text input saved as title
    'Description' as text area saved as description
    'Category' as dropdown with ['IT', 'HR', 'Facilities', 'Finance'] saved as category
    'Priority' as dropdown with ['Low', 'Normal', 'High', 'Urgent'] saved as priority
    'Your Name' as text input saved as submitter
    
    button 'Submit Request':
      if title is empty or submitter is empty:
        show alert 'Title and Name are required'
      else:
        send {title: title, description: description, category: category, priority: priority, submitter: submitter} as a new record to '/api/requests'
        show alert 'Request submitted successfully'
        set title to ''
        set description to ''
        set category to 'IT'
        set priority to 'Normal'
        set submitter to ''

page 'Triage Queue' at '/triage':
  requires login
  
  new_requests = []
  all_requests = []
  
  on page load:
    get new_requests from '/api/requests/new'
    get all_requests from '/api/requests'
  
  section 'New Requests':
    display new_requests as table
  
  section 'All Requests':
    display all_requests as table
